title: peyrousea
side_effects: Peyrousea can have side effects if used improperly, such as increased heart rate and blood pressure, which can lead to cardiovascular issues and even heart failure in severe cases. Ingesting excessive amounts of the plant's compounds can also cause vomiting, diarrhea, and abdominal pain, resulting in dehydration and electrolyte imbalances. Some individuals may experience hallucinations, delusions, and altered mental states due to the plant's psychoactive properties, leading to confusion and disorientation. Furthermore, long-term use or abuse of peyrousea can lead to physical dependence and addiction.
